mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If the annualized growth rate of GDPC1 is positive for two consecutive quarters, yet the S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below a certain threshold signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which drives corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 5.5% and the S&P 500 subsequently delivers negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy relative to price pressures, which typically supports equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accommodative policy
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL shows sustained acceleration while FEDFUNDS remains low, yet S&P 500 5-day returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand, which is a key driver of corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns, especially during periods of economic expansion.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth exceeds 4% annualized during an economic expansion, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of these instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, signaling healthy economic expansion and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent positive growth for two consecutive quarters, yet S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy labor market and growing economy, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS consistently adds over 100,000 jobs per month for three consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of these instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts signals weakening demand and potential economic contraction, which negatively impacts construction-related industries and broader economic sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% on a 3-month average basis, yet S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low and inflation is accelerating, it signals an overheating economy, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: If UNRATE is below 5.0% and CPIAUCSL shows sustained acceleration above 3.0% annualized, yet S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such periods.
